title: FCoP Agent
description: Abstraction 1 of 7 — Agent. Lifecycle + identity of an agent participating in an FCoP project. Per ADR-0015 §abstractions and ADR-0020 §roles, an Agent has an identity (code), a layer (worker/governance — not admin, see §boundary), and a capability bundle (can/cannot). This schema defines what a single agent record in `fcop.json` looks like, plus the ephemeral 'session' identity used by Failure recovery. Schema version is 1.0.0; field set is frozen for v1.x per ADR-0003 stability charter.

code.description: Stable role code (per ADR-0020). Uppercase, 1-16 chars, A-Z 0-9 only. Examples: PM, DEV, QA, OPS, ME, ADMIN. The code, not the human label, is the routing key in TASK/REPORT filenames.

label.description: Human-readable label (per ADR-0020). Free text, any language. Used in UIs and logs; not used for routing.

layer.description: Capability bundle shorthand (per ADR-0020 §layer). 'worker' is the default for code-producing agents; 'governance' for review-only agents; 'admin' MUST NOT appear in fcop.json — admin is reserved for human operators and runtime MUST reject programmatic creation of admin agents (ADR-0020 §validation rule 1).

can.description: Explicit allow-list of capability tokens (per ADR-0020). Overrides the layer default bundle when present. See boundary.schema.json for the full v1.0 capability vocabulary.

cannot.description: Explicit deny-list of capability tokens (per ADR-0020). Takes precedence over both `can` and the layer default. Use to subtract capabilities from a layer bundle without rewriting the whole bundle.

session_id.description: Ephemeral session identifier (per ADR-0019 §session-recovery-hook). Format is implementation-defined; the reference implementation uses '<task_id>:<agent_code>'. Not stored in fcop.json — only present on the in-memory Agent object during a live session.
